title: DockerAdapter compat-only-not-default selection enforcement + repo lint
goal: Lock in the spec §3.5.3 'DockerAdapter selected only when operator explicitly opts in (no implicit fallback)' rule with both runtime enforcement AND a repo-wide lint that prevents a future MT from accidentally setting Docker as the default. Pairs with MT-043's selection policy; this MT adds the discipline and the audit trail.
expected_diff_shape: New repo check .GOV/roles_shared/checks/docker-not-default-adapter-check.mjs that scans `bootstrap.rs` and any settings.* default to verify default_adapter_id is never "docker"; new settings schema enforcement under src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/settings_schema.rs defining the allowed values for `settings.sandbox.docker_explicit_opt_in` (bool, default false) and `settings.sandbox.default_adapter` (enum, default "wsl2_podman", value "docker" is REJECTED by the validator); tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/sandbox_compat_only_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: Selection policy + DockerAdapter + KERNEL-003 refactor merged.
implementation_notes: settings_schema.rs: `pub struct SandboxSettings { pub default_adapter: SandboxDefaultAdapterChoice, pub docker_explicit_opt_in: bool }` where `pub enum SandboxDefaultAdapterChoice { Wsl2Podman, WindowsNativeJail }` — NOTE: enum does NOT contain Docker, so by Rust typing the operator cannot set default_adapter="docker" via deserialization (a JSON value of "docker" returns a typed serde error). Add `impl Default for SandboxSettings { fn default() -> Self { SandboxSettings { default_adapter: SandboxDefaultAdapterChoice::Wsl2Podman, docker_explicit_opt_in: false } } }`. The bootstrap in MT-048 reads SandboxSettings via settings_schema and feeds the default_adapter choice into SandboxAdapterRegistry::new — this MT updates that wiring. docker-not-default-adapter-check.mjs: a Node-side repo check that (1) reads bootstrap.rs and asserts `SandboxAdapterRegistry::new(AdapterId("docker"))` or string "docker" never appears as the default arg in a constructor call; (2) reads .GOV/settings/**/*.json (any operator settings defaults) and verifies `sandbox.default_adapter` is never "docker"; (3) emits a structured failure row JSONL on stderr for each violation. Wire the check into `just gov-check` (recipe addition; if cluster A.1 owns justfile, coordinate). Tests: (a) deserializing `{ "default_adapter": "docker" }` fails with a typed serde error; (b) docker-not-default-adapter-check.mjs passes on the current repo state; (c) docker-not-default-adapter-check.mjs fails on a synthetic fixture where bootstrap.rs has been mutated to default to docker (negative test, exit 2); (d) runtime test: selection.select() called with a ProcessSpec preferring Docker AND docker_explicit_opt_in==false returns SandboxSelectionFailure::DockerNotExplicitlyOptedIn (preserving MT-043 behavior; this MT re-asserts).
risk_if_missed: Without the lint, a future MT could silently flip Docker to default (settings change in a low-traffic file or a constructor default arg edit) and KERNEL-004's 3-adapter posture collapses back to KERNEL-003's Docker-only world.
validator_focus: (a) Enum-level enforcement (Rust type system rejects Docker as default); (b) repo check passes on current state AND fails on the negative fixture; (c) wired into gov-check; (d) MT-043 runtime guard re-validated.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Rust enum lacks a Docker variant for SandboxDefaultAdapterChoice — type-level enforcement is the primary control.
Negative fixture test for the repo check proves the lint actually fires on a Docker-default mutation.
Wired into `just gov-check` so the lint is part of every PR's build-time gate (HBR-INT-001 surface).

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: settings_schema.rs implements SandboxDefaultAdapterChoice as a Rust enum that lacks any Docker variant - type-level rejection per the contract; serde deny_unknown_fields adds defense-in-depth. docker-not-default-adapter-check.mjs scans bootstrap.rs and .GOV/settings JSON for Docker defaults with full comment-stripping. sandbox_compat_only_tests.rs covers the type rejection, the WindowsNativeJail rejection, unknown_field rejection, and runtime-side select() guard. Bootstrap uses disallowed_default_adapter_reason() to belt-and-suspenders block Docker-as-default at registry construction.

Bootstrap uses disallowed_default_adapter_reason() to belt-and-suspenders block Docker-as-default at registry construction.
